name: chkrootkit
title: chkrootkit
summary: chkrootkit - Scan the system for signs of rootkits
description:
rootkit detector
The chkrootkit security scanner searches for signs that the system is
infected with a 'rootkit'. Rootkits are a form of malware that seek
to exploit security flaws to grant unauthorised access to a
computer or its services, generally for malicious purposes.
.
chkrootkit can identify signs of over 70 different rootkits (see the
project's website for a list).
.
Please note that an automated tool like chkrootkit can never
guarantee a system is uncompromised. Nor does every report always
signify a genuine problem: human judgement and further investigation
will always be needed to assure the security of your system.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use chkrootkit because chkrootkit - Scan the system for signs of rootkits.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use chkrootkit as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chkrootkit [OPTIONS] [TEST...]` as documented upstream for chkrootkit.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair chkrootkit with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `chkrootkit --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
